issues.title: Unable to startup the Chat App when setting up the JdbcChatMemoryRepository with Vertex AI model
issues.body:
When I'm trying to setup the chat memory with mysql datastore, getting unrelated errors during application startup.

I'm using spring boot 3.4.6, spring ai 1.0.0 version with jdk21

Here is the sample reproducible app
https://github.com/maradanasai/demo-springai.git

comments.body: Hi @maradanasai I clone the sample project you gave and tried to run it, but it started normally on my machine (My env: MacOS15.4 , JDK 21) .I guess this is because of a conflict in your dependencies, by checking `./gradlew dependencies` I found that your dependencies depend on two different versions of `protobuf-java` (4.26.1 and 3.25.5), maybe you can try to resolve the dependency conflict and the problem might be solved!

Sure, thanks for the prompt response @sunyuhan1998 

Also, it is noticed that it's working fine in spring boot 3.5.0

issues.title: Spring.ai.model.chat=none configuration does not take effect
issues.body:
Why did I set the configuration item 'spring.ai.model.chat=none' in my properties, but still perform an apikey check during service startup and throw an exception 'OpenAI API key must be set'.
If it is still necessary to configure the apikey to properties, is it unreasonable to overwrite the apikey when using mute for multi chat model configuration in the official documentation

comments.body:
There are many models in the `spring-ai-openai` module.

The error probably occurred in a model other than `chat`.
To prevent this in all cases, try setting it up as follows:

```
spring.ai.model.chat=none
spring.ai.model.audio.speech=none
spring.ai.model.audio.transcription=none
spring.ai.model.image=none
spring.ai.model.embedding=none
spring.ai.model.moderation=none
```

ok this is my code and properties options...
pom.xml I just import 
        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
            <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-web</artifactId>
        </dependency>
and
        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.springframework.ai</groupId>
            <artifactId>spring-ai-starter-model-openai</artifactId>
        </dependency>
and in my properties I just set spring.ai.model.chat=none item
then i run my application that tips 'OpenAI API key must be set'.

I debugged the auto configuration class and found that the error occurred in the speech auto configuration. When retrieving the apikey from the properties, an error was reported indicating that the corresponding apikey needs to be filled in. I think you are right, and it needs to be set for each model, such as the one you mentioned
spring.ai.model.chat=none
spring.ai.model.audio.speech=none
spring.ai.model.audio.transcription=none
spring.ai.model.image=none
spring.ai.model.embedding=none
spring.ai.model.moderation=none
But I only saw spring.ai.model.chat=none from the official documentation, which caused the subsequent auto configuration classes to load as well, and then prompted me that I didn't have an apikey
Anyway, thank you for your answer

Yes, it would be nice to add that to the documentation

If you'd like to contribute, feel free to give it a try.

issues.title: AutoConfiguration of ChromaVectorStore lacks tenant and database definition
issues.body:
**Bug description**
The problem is in `auto-configurations/vector-stores/spring-ai-autoconfigure-vector-store-chroma/src/main/java/org/springframework/ai/vectorstore/chroma/autoconfigure/ChromaVectorStoreAutoConfiguration.java`

The `vectorStore` bean definition does not set the tenantName and databaseName (only collectionName). 

The necessary values are in `storeProperties` (just like with collectionName).

(I think these properties are also not documented?)

I found this when my application crashed and the logs showed this URL: `http://localhost:8008/api/v2/tenants/SpringAiTenant/databases/SpringAiDatabase/collections/knowledge`

The tenant and database are wrong even though I set them in my application.yml

The collection name is correct: 'knowledge'

I think the code should look something like this:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/fa53da58-9608-4c4f-b6d8-55f03d029e0e)

comments.body: Indeed, it seems that the `ChromaVectorStoreProperties` of `ChromaVectorStore` originally had only the `collectionName` property in its early version, and later added `databaseName` and `tenantName`, but the `AutoConfiguration` was not updated accordingly. I've submitted a PR to fix this issue. Thank you for reporting it!
